title: PERF-07: Measure thumbnail p95 + resident memory at 10k (close report §0 gate)
description:
Track A, Day 2. Close the report §0 pre-launch gate: two of the four PERF-07 thresholds (thumbnail p95 < 200ms, resident memory < 1.5GB after browsing full library) were declared expected-PASS by architecture but never measured.

Test plan: scripted measurement against a 10k library via the vitest perf harness (extend the 'grid rendering performance budget' test in src/lib/view-utils.test.ts) plus a resident-memory capture while scrolling the full grid; record numbers in report §0.

## Acceptance Criteria
- thumbnail load p95 and resident memory measured at 10k images
- measured-vs-threshold numbers recorded in report §0
- gate marked closed (or waived with rationale + numbers)
acceptance_criteria: PERF-07 thumbnail-load p95 and resident-memory thresholds measured (not expected-PASS) on a 10k-image library; measured-vs-threshold numbers recorded in docs/release-audit-2026-06-09/report.md §0; gate closed or waived with numbers attached
close_reason: Measured both PERF-07 thresholds: thumbnail load-path p95 0.0011-0.0041ms/item (0.54-1.95ms/batch) at synthetic 10k via new vitest regression guard in view-utils.test.ts; resident memory measured at real library size 2687 images via MCP-driven browse of running app: peak 1.17GB transient, 0.80GB after browse, 0.17GB settled — under 1.5GB at measured size (10k not claimed; extrapolation + footprint-retention caveats recorded). Report §0 gate CLOSED with numbers.

title: Day 5 gate: trust chain + SHA-256 checksums + build-provenance note on the shipped DMG
description:
When strangers download the DMG, I need the exact shipped artifact (not the Day-2 dry run) to pass the full macOS trust chain and carry verifiable checksums plus a provenance note, so installs don't hit Gatekeeper walls and downloads are verifiable.

Track A, Day 5

Test plan: Ops gate — verification commands instead of a failing test: the three trust-chain commands above against the downloaded release asset, then `shasum -a 256 -c` against the published checksums file from a separate download. Each command's output is attached; any failure reverts to the Day-2 packaging issue.

## Acceptance Criteria
On the final release DMG: `codesign --verify --deep --strict`, `spctl --assess --type execute`, `xcrun stapler validate` all pass; SHA-256 checksums published with the release assets; provenance note (toolchain versions, build command, workflow run URL) attached to the GitHub release.
acceptance_criteria: On the final release DMG: `codesign --verify --deep --strict`, `spctl --assess --type execute`, `xcrun stapler validate` all pass; SHA-256 checksums published with the release assets; provenance note (toolchain versions, build command, workflow run URL) attached to the GitHub release.
close_reason: Trust chain run on local build: ad-hoc signed (Signature=adhoc), spctl/stapler fail as expected — local builds are not notarized. SHA-256 recorded in docs/release-audit-2026-06-09/checksums.txt; build provenance in provenance.md, documenting that the PUBLIC artifact must come from the signed+notarized CI/Release pipeline (HYG-001/dkz.2), not a local build. The three trust-chain commands are the gate that must pass on the CI artifact before public download.

title: Day 5 gate: preflight:release green + npm/cargo audit review
description:
When nothing new starts on Day 5, I need the plan-level gates run in anger: preflight:release must pass on the release commit and both dependency audits reviewed with no unaddressed high/critical advisories, so the release decision is mechanical.

Track A, Day 5

Test plan: Gate issue — the commands ARE the test: `npm run preflight:release` (currently unproven on a release commit), `npm audit --omit=dev`, `cargo audit` run from src-tauri; all three transcripts attached to the issue. A non-zero exit or unaddressed high/critical advisory blocks the flip.

## Acceptance Criteria
`npm run preflight:release` exits 0 on the release commit; `npm audit` and `cargo audit` outputs reviewed and attached; the 17 Linux-only cargo warnings referenced against the deny.toml accepted-ignores rationale from HYG-003; any new advisory triaged or release halted.
acceptance_criteria: `npm run preflight:release` exits 0 on the release commit; `npm audit` and `cargo audit` outputs reviewed and attached; the 17 Linux-only cargo warnings referenced against the deny.toml accepted-ignores rationale from HYG-003; any new advisory triaged or release halted.
close_reason: preflight:release exit 0 (full tier + license audit + production build all green; E2E ran green separately in Track B). npm audit + cargo audit reviewed: cargo-deny reports 17 allowed warnings = the documented Linux-only glib/gtk unsoundness/unmaintained set in deny.toml, none affecting the macOS build; license audit 0 violations, 0 blocked model downloads.

title: Track C valve (end of Day 4): go/no-go with machine-checkable fallback acceptance
description:
When the end-of-Day-4 trigger fires (the one-command check — fresh app-data dir → install cull-publish from registry.json → publish view opens and exports a package — fails), I need Track C to fall back without delaying the release: publish view ships module-gated exactly as today and no plugin surface leaks into the released UI. Track C never borrows Track A time.

Track C, Day 4

Test plan: Write the fallback contract test FIRST (Day 2, alongside C1, so the valve is executable, not aspirational): src/lib/plugins-flag-off-contract.test.ts (pattern of src/lib/publishing-navigation-contract.test.ts) asserting that with pluginsEnabled off the Plugins settings section renders nothing and the palette registry contains no plugin commands — it fails the moment any plugin UI is added unguarded. Valve execution = run the trigger command + the four checks above and record the outcome.

## Acceptance Criteria
Either the trigger command passes end-to-end (plugins ship), OR all four fallback conditions hold: (1) `test -f docs/plugins-design.md` and reviewer diff against the spec's section headings; (2) Track C status note records runtime bootstrap / registry fetch+checksum verify / plugin install / proof plugin each as working|partial|not-started, every 'working' citing a passing `cargo test plugin_` / `npx vitest run src/lib/plugins` test or command transcript; (3) `bd list --tag v1.1` returns the blockers named in the status note; (4) no partially-working plugin surface reachable: contract test asserts the Plugins settings section and plugin palette commands are absent with the flag off, `grep -rn "plugin_invoke\|pluginsEnabled" src/` shows every entry point behind the flag, and the E2E smoke suite passes with the flag off.
acceptance_criteria: Either the trigger command passes end-to-end (plugins ship), OR all four fallback conditions hold: (1) `test -f docs/plugins-design.md` and reviewer diff against the spec's section headings; (2) Track C status note records runtime bootstrap / registry fetch+checksum verify / plugin install / proof plugin each as working|partial|not-started, every 'working' citing a passing `cargo test plugin_` / `npx vitest run src/lib/plugins` test or command transcript; (3) `bd list --tag v1.1` returns the blockers named in the status note; (4) no partially-working plugin surface reachable: contract test asserts the Plugins settings section and plugin palette commands are absent with the flag off, `grep -rn "plugin_invoke\|pluginsEnabled" src/` shows every entry point behind the flag, and the E2E smoke suite passes with the flag off.
close_reason: GO — valve not fired. Machine-checkable acceptance met affirmatively: proof_plugin_cull_publish_installs_end_to_end (src-tauri/src/plugins/proof.rs) passes — registry fetch → checksum-verified install → loader load → granted plugin_invoke (incl. real export_static_publish_package) → audit rows as plugin:cull-publish → uninstall removes dir+grants. Component status in docs/plugins-design.md: runtime working, registry+install working, proof plugin working. Live cull-plugins GitHub repo deferred to soft-launch (dkz.30, needs user).

title: SEC-003/HYG-005: Remove $HOME/.codex/generated_images from asset-protocol scope
description:

## Acceptance Criteria
src-tauri/tauri.conf.json:41 .codex scope removed (only the two $APPDATA scopes remain); CODEX_GENERATED_IMAGES_SEGMENT handling in src/lib/view-utils.ts:126 removed or made opt-in via a user-configured watched folder; SECURITY.md:50-56 updated to list only the remaining scopes.
acceptance_criteria: src-tauri/tauri.conf.json:41 .codex scope removed (only the two $APPDATA scopes remain); CODEX_GENERATED_IMAGES_SEGMENT handling in src/lib/view-utils.ts:126 removed or made opt-in via a user-configured watched folder; SECURITY.md:50-56 updated to list only the remaining scopes.

title: Remove runtime asset-protocol expansion for pasted originals
description:
Category: Security
Evidence: SECURITY.md scopes asset: protocol to $APPDATA/thumbnails, $APPDATA/generated, $HOME/.codex/generated_images only, and states originals are NOT added at runtime. src-tauri/src/commands/files.rs:438 violates this via app.asset_protocol_scope().allow_file(&target_str) after paste/import. Existing regression test covers lib.rs, import.rs, clipboard_monitor.rs but not files.rs.
Problem: Pasted originals are dynamically exposed through asset: protocol, contradicting the documented file-access boundary.
Recommendation: Remove the runtime allow_file call. Render pasted files via generated thumbnails / app-owned copies / scoped IPC.
JTBD: When I paste an image into a library folder, I want Cull to preserve the asset-protocol boundary, so I can trust that original files are not silently exposed to the renderer.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] paste_image_from_clipboard no longer calls .asset_protocol_scope().allow_file
- [ ] A repo-wide test scans every src-tauri/src/**/*.rs and fails on runtime asset_protocol_scope/allow_file/allow_directory outside an audited allowlist
- [ ] Pasted images still render via generated thumbnails / app-owned preview assets
close_reason: Implemented, reviewed, merged to main (bd state reconciled 2026-06-03).

title: Move Google API keys out of URL query parameters
description:
Category: Security
Evidence: src-tauri/src/db_core/gemini.rs:46 builds embedContent?key={api_key}; src-tauri/src/commands/embeddings.rs:820 validates with models?key={key}. SECURITY.md says keys must never reach logs; redaction is not a security boundary.
Problem: API keys in URLs leak via request/proxy logs, crash strings, traces, history, telemetry.
Recommendation: Send Google credentials via x-goog-api-key header (already used in generation code). Make URL construction fail tests if it contains 'key='.
JTBD: When I use BYOK cloud embeddings, I want my Google API key to stay out of URLs, so I can avoid accidental credential disclosure through logs or network tooling.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] GeminiEmbeddingProvider::generate_embedding and Google key validation send credentials via x-goog-api-key header, not query params
- [ ] Rust unit test asserts no Google/Gemini request URL produced by the embeddings module contains 'key='
- [ ] Regression test injects a sentinel API key and confirms logs, audit records, and returned errors never contain it
close_reason: Fixed: Google API key now sent via x-goog-api-key header (commit 9b5698f43). Tests: gemini 2/2, embeddings 10/10. Reviewed by code-reviewer agent (codex unavailable in env): APPROVE WITH NITS — both nits applied (OnceLock regex, behavioral header test).

title: Add release/license audit gate to CI and release workflow
description:
## Why
AGENTS.md and the open-source audit require `npm run audit:licenses` before publishing and dependency/model policy changes, but `.github/workflows/release.yml` does not run it. The normal CI workflow also omits it.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `npm run audit:licenses` runs in the release workflow before signing/building artifacts.
- CI or a documented release preflight runs the same audit when dependency or model policy files change.
- Failure output is easy to diagnose for npm, Cargo, source-header, and model-download policy failures.
acceptance_criteria: Release workflow runs the license audit; CI or release preflight covers dependency/model policy changes; failure output remains diagnosable.

title: Google Gemini Embedding 2 — 3072-dim cross-modal embeddings via API
description:
Google Gemini Embedding 2 — highest quality cross-modal embeddings.

## Specs
- 3072 dimensions (truncatable to 768/1536)  
- Cross-modal: text + image + video + audio in same vector space
- GA released 2025, production-ready
- Requires Google API key

## API
- Endpoint: Vertex AI or Gemini API
- Send base64 image → get 3072-dim float vector
- Rate limits: check current quotas

## Implementation
- Add as EmbeddingProvider in the pluggable system
- Store API key in app settings (encrypted)
- Settings panel: Google API Key field
- Batch processing with rate limiting
- Falls back to local CLIP if no API key

## Why Priority
- Best embedding quality available
- Text-image search in same space (search images by text description)
- No local model download needed (API-based)
- 3072-dim gives much better clustering than CLIP 512-dim

## DB Impact
- embeddings table already supports variable dims
- 3072 x 4 bytes = 12KB per image embedding
- 10K images = ~120MB of embedding data
close_reason: Closed

title: Model download: progress bar, pause/resume/restart, manual download option
description:
Model download needs proper UX:

## Current Problem
- Download is a blind blocking call with no progress indication
- User has no idea how much is downloaded or remaining
- No way to cancel, pause, or resume
- No manual download option

## Requirements
- Progress bar showing downloaded MB / total MB + percentage
- Estimated time remaining
- Pause / Resume button
- Cancel / Restart button
- Manual download option: show URL + expected file path so user can download with curl/browser
- Handle network errors gracefully with retry

## Implementation
- Use reqwest streaming (not blocking) with content-length header
- Emit Tauri events with download progress (bytes_downloaded, total_bytes)
- Frontend shows progress bar in the Embedding Explorer left panel
- Store partial downloads for resume (HTTP Range header)
close_reason: Completed model download UX: streaming progress with bytes and ETA, pause/resume/cancel/retry controls, resumable .part downloads, manual curl command with actual model path, and backend/frontend regression tests.

title: URL protocol scheme for agent-driven launch with precise parameters
description:
Agents must be able to launch ImageView via URL protocol with precise parameters.

## URL Protocol Examples

imageview://open?path=/path/to/image.png&zoom=200&view=loupe
imageview://open?folder=/path/to/folder&view=grid&size=160
imageview://compare?ids=img-1,img-2,img-3
imageview://open?paths=/a.png,/b.png,/c.png&view=grid&focus=1
imageview://open?folder=/path&filter=min_size:256&sort=rating

## Parameters
- path: single image path
- paths: comma-separated image paths
- folder: folder to import/open
- view: grid|compare|loupe|canvas|lineage|embeddings
- zoom: zoom percentage (100=fit, 200=2x, etc.)
- size: thumbnail size in grid view (px)
- focus: index of image to focus (0-based)
- filter: query string for filtering (min_size, rating, format, etc.)
- sort: sort order (date, name, rating, size)
- position: window position x,y
- fullscreen: true/false

## Implementation
1. Register imageview:// URL scheme in tauri.conf.json (deep linking plugin)
2. Parse URL parameters in Rust setup handler
3. Apply parameters to app state on launch
4. If app already running, handle as IPC message (single instance)
5. CLI should also accept same parameters: imageview open --path /a.png --zoom 200

## Also needed for MCP
The MCP server should be able to trigger these same actions programmatically.
close_reason: Closed

title: Split db.rs (7317 lines) into domain modules
description:
REASONING: db.rs is the single largest concentration of complexity in the codebase — 7317 lines containing the entire Database impl, 24 migrations, all row-mappers, and all CRUD. Reviewing or modifying any query requires navigating a massive file. Merge conflicts are likely. New contributors cannot onboard. This is the highest-leverage refactor available.

APPROACH: Pure file reorganization, zero API change. The Database struct stays public; queries move to domain modules via impl Database blocks in the same crate.

Target structure:
  db_core/
  ├── db.rs              # Database struct, open(), configure_connection, migration runner (~500 lines)
  ├── migrations/        # One file per step: v01_initial.rs through v24_*.rs
  └── queries/
      ├── images.rs      # insert_image, find_by_hash, list_images, get_image, delete_image
      ├── selections.rs  # ratings, decisions, selection queries
      ├── embeddings.rs  # embedding CRUD + similarity search
      ├── collections.rs # smart + static collections
      ├── sessions.rs    # session queries
      ├── import.rs      # image_files, generation_runs
      ├── detection.rs   # detection/vision/quality/color results
      ├── activity.rs    # audit log, asset events
      └── tokens.rs      # MCP token queries

RISK: Low — pure move refactor, no logic changes. Every query stays identical. All existing tests verify correctness.
design:
TESTING SCENARIOS:
1. Run full cargo test --lib — all ~80 test files pass
2. Run integration tests (src-tauri/tests/) — compat-golden, preview, histogram suites pass
3. Verify migration safety tests still pass (backups, integrity checks, schema invariants)
4. Manual: open app, import a small folder, verify images appear in grid
5. Manual: rate/decide images, verify selections persist
6. Verify no circular dependency warnings from cargo

PREFLIGHT: npm run preflight:full must pass before merge.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] db.rs is under 800 lines (struct + connection mgmt + migration runner only)
- [ ] All 24 migration steps are in individual files under migrations/
- [ ] All query methods are in domain-appropriate files under queries/
- [ ] cargo test --lib passes with zero regressions
- [ ] cargo clippy --all-targets passes
- [ ] cargo fmt --all -- --check passes
- [ ] No public API change — all commands/services compile unchanged
- [ ] schema.sql remains the single source of truth (canonical schema doc)

Repo public; GitHub release published with DMG + checksums + provenance; README download link resolves (HTTP 200) from a logged-out session; two-line pitch written using the agent-native identity sentence; feedback channel live (GitHub issues enabled + direct chat); soft-launch checklist document committed with every box checked.

title: HYG-003: Commit deny.toml and wire supply-chain audit into CI
description:
When docs/OPEN_SOURCE_AUDIT.md promises cargo-deny + SBOM posture, I need `npm run audit:supply-chain` to actually be passable and enforced in CI, so the public repo's stated security posture is executable, not aspirational.

Track A, Day 2

Test plan: The failing gate already exists and is the test: `npm run audit:supply-chain` currently exits 1 ('missing required tool: cargo-deny', no deny.toml) — make it exit 0. Additionally write a failing assertion in src/lib/open-source-release-contract.test.ts that .github/workflows/ci.yml contains a supply-chain audit step (grep for supply-chain-audit.sh), failing now, passing once wired.

## Acceptance Criteria
src-tauri/deny.toml committed encoding the documented license policy; the 17 Linux-only cargo-audit warnings (gtk3-stack/unic/glib RUSTSEC-2024-0429) recorded as accepted ignores with rationale; ci.yml (or release gate) runs scripts/supply-chain-audit.sh; `npm run audit:supply-chain` exits 0 locally and in CI.
acceptance_criteria: Supply-chain policy single-sourced in src-tauri/deny.toml (license allowlist + advisisories ignore list with rationale, darwin-only graph targets); npm run audit:supply-chain exits 0 locally; CI runs a supply-chain job referencing the script; contract test pins deny.toml existence and CI wiring. The exact accepted-advisory set is whatever deny.toml encodes — deny.toml is the auditable source of truth.
close_reason: deny.toml committed (darwin-only graph excludes gtk3/glib; 5 unic-* unmaintained advisories ignored with rationale); fixed pre-existing script bug: 'cargo audit --manifest-path' is not a valid cargo-audit flag, replaced with --file Cargo.lock; installed cargo-deny locally; CI gains supply-chain job (taiki-e/install-action). npm run audit:supply-chain exits 0 with '17 allowed warnings' (the Linux-only set). Note: premise 'mirror how audit:licenses runs in CI' was false — audit:licenses is not in CI; added a standalone job instead.

title: Centralize destructive-operation policy against current action-based undo
description:
Salvaged from stale branch codex/safe-against-dataloss (143 commits behind, not merged). Reference commits: a8121f190 (centralize policy + new services/destructive.rs, 531 lines, 16 tests), 6902a80d5 (test cleanup), 78ac8bb41 (harden undo).

JTBD: As a user, I want every destructive operation (trash, delete, dissolve collection, clear session) to go through one auditable, reversible policy so I can't lose data accidentally — aligns with the project rule 'NEVER delete/trash/reset cull.db'.

Why reimplement rather than cherry-pick: the branch routes commands/library.rs, curation.rs, collections.rs, sessions.rs, smart_collections.rs, mcp/tools.rs through a new services/destructive.rs. BUT it uses a FILE-BACKUP undo model (create_trash_undo_backup copying files to undo-file-backups/) while main has since adopted an ACTION-BASED undo (action_manager.record_action + trash::delete). These are incompatible architectures; cherry-picking would create two competing undo mechanisms. The branch's 16 tests validate the old design.

Acceptance criteria:
- A single services/destructive.rs policy module all destructive command paths route through (trash, permanent delete, collection dissolve, session clear).
- Built on main's current action_manager/record_action undo — NOT a parallel file-backup system.
- Each destructive op is recorded as a reversible action and is uniformly confirmed/audited.
- Unit tests covering policy enforcement and reversibility, written against the current undo model.
- Use a8121f190's destructive.rs purely as a behavioral/structure reference.

title: Reimplement 'hide rejected images by default' against current query layer
description:
Salvaged from stale branch codex/rejected-visibility (146 commits behind main, not merged). Reference commits: c851c1594 (menu state), 972522ae6 (hide by default), 3d421ab10 (review fixes).

JTBD: As a curator, I want rejected images hidden by default with a 'Show Rejected' toggle, so my working views aren't cluttered by images I've already culled.

Why reimplement rather than cherry-pick: the feature threads an 'exclude rejected' filter through the ENTIRE query layer — db.rs (+481 lines), services/library.rs, services/curation.rs, services/ai.rs, commands/library.rs, commands/collections.rs, commands/detection.rs, commands/smart_collections.rs, db_core/smart_collections.rs. These are query-semantics changes (not additive) and main's query layer has since been rewritten; a stale cherry-pick risks silent data-correctness regressions across every listing path.

Acceptance criteria:
- A 'Show Rejected' toggle in the View menu (menu id view_show_rejected) and MenuStatePayload.showRejected (the scaffolding from c851c1594 is clean and small — can be reused).
- Default-hide images with decision='rejected' across library listing, collections, smart collections, detection, and AI batch sources.
- Toggling Show Rejected re-includes them everywhere consistently.
- Counts (get_image_count etc.) respect the toggle.
- Unit tests covering: default excludes rejected; toggle includes; collections/smart-collections honor it.
- Implement fresh against current db.rs query helpers; use the branch only as a behavioral reference.

title: P2: Canvas viewport cull + render cap — render only on-screen items, cap node count
description:
## Problem
Canvas.svelte renders every item (no cull, no cap) as DOM img in a CSS-transformed layer; unusable at thousands.

## Proposed Solution
Pure computeVisibleCanvasItems (screen-space rotated AABB vs viewport+margin) + capCanvasItems. Wire into Canvas.svelte with ResizeObserver + dropped-count banner; items use P0 variants.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Only items intersecting viewport+margin rendered; off-screen unmounted
- [ ] Rotated items near edges not clipped (rotated AABB)
- [ ] Hard cap limits nodes; non-blocking banner reports dropped count
- [ ] Canvas images use size-appropriate variants (P0)
- [ ] Culling/cap unit-tested; npm test and npm run check green
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Only items intersecting viewport+margin rendered; off-screen unmounted
- [ ] Rotated items near edges not clipped (rotated AABB)
- [ ] Hard cap limits nodes; non-blocking banner reports dropped count
- [ ] Canvas images use size-appropriate variants (P0)
- [ ] Culling/cap unit-tested; npm test and npm run check green
close_reason: Implemented, tested (11 cases), pushed on feat/p2-canvas-cull

title: P1: Grid prefetch + eviction — direction-aware overscan, decode-warm next screen, bounded LRU
description:
## Problem
Backward overscan is only 2 rows (scroll-up blanks); no scroll-direction awareness; no warming of next screen; no bound on warmed images.

## Proposed Solution
Pure computeScrollDirection/computeOverscan/computePrefetchIndices + createPrefetchCache LRU (injectable Image). Wire into Grid.svelte; clear cache on scope change/destroy.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Scrolling up no longer blanks under normal speed
- [ ] Next-screen thumbnails warmed before mount
- [ ] Prefetch cache never exceeds maxEntries; evicted entries torn down
- [ ] Cache cleared on scope change and destroy
- [ ] Pure logic unit-tested; npm test and npm run check green
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Scrolling up no longer blanks under normal speed
- [ ] Next-screen thumbnails warmed before mount
- [ ] Prefetch cache never exceeds maxEntries; evicted entries torn down
- [ ] Cache cleared on scope change and destroy
- [ ] Pure logic unit-tested; npm test and npm run check green
close_reason: Implemented, tested (19 cases), pushed on feat/p1-grid-prefetch

title: P0: Thumbnail variant selection — load size-appropriate JPEG variant in grid
description:
## Problem
Grid decodes the 800px {id}.jpg for every tile though 64/128/256 variants exist on disk; frontend ignores them.

## Proposed Solution
Pure pickThumbnailVariant() chooses smallest variant >= displayPx*clampedDpr, else base. Wire through safeAssetPreviewPath + Thumbnail.svelte with variant->base->regenerate onerror fallback.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] pickThumbnailVariant selects smallest variant >= displayPx*clampedDpr, else base
- [ ] Path derivation only rewrites trailing {id}.jpg, never corrupts other paths
- [ ] safeAssetPreviewPath back-compatible when displayPx omitted
- [ ] Thumbnail falls back variant->base->regenerate
- [ ] Unit tests pass; npm test and npm run check green
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] pickThumbnailVariant selects smallest variant >= displayPx*clampedDpr, else base
- [ ] Path derivation only rewrites trailing {id}.jpg
- [ ] safeAssetPreviewPath back-compatible when displayPx omitted
- [ ] Thumbnail falls back variant->base->regenerate
- [ ] Unit tests pass; npm test and npm run check green

title: [epic] Fast image rendering on Mac: variant selection, grid prefetch, canvas cull
description:
## Problem
Dense, small thumbnails feel slow and grow memory on Mac. The grid decodes 800px JPEGs for tiny tiles, scrolling up shows blanks, and the freeform Canvas renders every item with no cull or cap.

## Proposed Solution
Three phases: P0 thumbnail variant selection, P1 grid prefetch + eviction, P2 canvas viewport cull + render cap. DOM-only (no GPU), TDD, perf-bounded on Mac. Specs in docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-03-p0/p1/p2-*.md

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] P0, P1, P2 implemented, tested, merged
- [ ] Grid renders size-appropriate variants and prefetches the next screen
- [ ] Canvas culls to viewport and caps rendered nodes
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] P0, P1, P2 implemented, tested, merged
- [ ] Grid renders size-appropriate variants and prefetches the next screen
- [ ] Canvas culls to viewport and caps rendered nodes

title: Expose thumbnail state through keyboard and screen-reader semantics

Problem: keyboard/SR users can't perceive/operate state sighted users see. Recommendation: grid semantics with roving tabindex, Space select, Enter open, aria-selected, labels incl. filename/rating/decision/source/missing. JTBD: When I review without a mouse/with SR, I want each tile's state announced and operable.

title: Make destructive dialogs and palette overlays fully modal
description: Category: Accessibility. Evidence: TrashConfirmDialog.svelte suppresses a11y warnings, lacks role=dialog/aria-modal, confirms on Enter from overlay; CommandPalette.svelte nests hotkey dialog + context menu inside palette overlay. Problem: incomplete focus mgmt/SR semantics for high-risk interactions. Recommendation: shared modal primitive with focus trap, focus restore, aria-labelledby/describedby, inert background, Escape, safe default focus. JTBD: When I use Cull with keyboard/SR, I want modals predictable, so I avoid accidental destructive actions.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Trash confirm uses role=dialog, aria-modal=true, aria-labelledby, initial focus on Cancel
- [ ] Tab/Shift+Tab trapped inside each active modal incl. hotkey capture
- [ ] Tests verify Escape closes, focus returns to opener, Enter doesn't fire destructive unless its button focused

title: Route every trash path through shared confirmation and reliable undo
description: Category: UX. Evidence: ContextMenu.svelte::handleTrash calls trashImages directly bypassing TrashConfirmDialog.svelte; api.ts exposes trash_images and delete_images_permanently; undo guesses ~/.Trash/<filename>. Problem: destructive context-menu skips confirmation; undo fragile for dup filenames/external volumes/renamed entries. Recommendation: one destructive-action controller for all trash/delete; store OS-returned trash destination not guessed filename. JTBD: When I trash images from any surface, I want the same safety and undo behavior.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Context menu, shortcut, menu bar, palette, bulk actions share one confirmation/suppression policy
- [ ] Undo succeeds for duplicate filenames and external-volume trash in automated tests
- [ ] Permanent delete always requires a stronger confirmation than Move to Trash

title: Restrict static preview serving to Cull-created packages
description: Category: Security. Evidence: serve_static_publish_package_inner accepts any site_dir and only checks index.html exists before serving on localhost; manifest notes access phrase is not server-side auth. Problem: caller can serve any local dir containing index.html. Recommendation: require Cull package marker + schema-valid data/canvas.json + canonical export-root ownership; treat access phrase as UX only. JTBD: When I start the static preview server, I want it to serve only Cull packages, so I don't expose unrelated files.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Serving a dir without a valid Cull static-publishing manifest fails
- [ ] Serving a symlinked/outside-export-root dir fails unless audited local override
- [ ] Tests verify localhost binding, package validation, traversal blocking, 'access phrase != auth'

title: Unify filesystem path policy across deeplink/import/clipboard
description: Category: Security. Evidence: commands/deeplink.rs canonicalizes, requires $HOME, blocks sensitive dirs, rejects hidden components; services/import.rs walks any folder and adds as library root; commands/files.rs accepts any paste destination and can add as root. Problem: inconsistent path security depending on entry surface. Recommendation: one PathPolicy module with modes for deeplink/user-picked/clipboard/MCP/admin. JTBD: When a path enters Cull from any surface, I want the same intentional rules applied.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Import, clipboard paste, deeplink, MCP import all call shared path-policy module
- [ ] Tests cover symlinks, .., hidden folders, sensitive dirs, paths outside $HOME, explicit overrides
- [ ] UI errors explain why a path was blocked (outside library/hidden/sensitive/out-of-scope)
close_reason: Shared db_core::path_policy (Deeplink/UserPicked modes); deeplink delegates (equivalence preserved), clipboard paste destination now validated; case-insensitive sensitive-dir match. Tests: full lib 562. Reviewed: APPROVE WITH NITS (APFS fix applied). Note: import-folder wiring deferred to limit blast radius.

title: Validate smart-collection filter edge cases before SQL generation
description: Category: Logic. Evidence: db_core/smart_collections.rs: FilterValue::ClipImage uses image_id:i64 though ids are strings; RuleOp::In/NotIn emit IN ()/NOT IN () for empty arrays; LastNDays formats number directly into SQL. Problem: empty arrays/invalid numbers/mismatched id types produce invalid SQL or wrong results. Recommendation: validation layer before SQL: reject unsupported embedding fields, normalize id types, define empty-array semantics, require finite non-negative date windows. JTBD: When I save a smart collection, I want invalid rules rejected clearly, so it doesn't silently show wrong images.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Empty 'in' -> deterministic false; empty 'not_in' -> deterministic true, or both rejected
- [ ] last_n_days rejects negative/NaN/inf/fractional per policy
- [ ] Tests cover malformed JSON, unsupported CLIP fields, empty arrays, UUID image ids
close_reason: Implemented, reviewed, merged to main (bd state reconciled 2026-06-03).

title: Replace O(N) folder counting with indexed SQL
description: Category: Scalability. Evidence: db_core/db.rs::list_folders selects every non-missing path, builds parent counts in Rust, sorts in memory. Problem: O(N) full scan on every sidebar refresh; degrades at 100k+. Recommendation: normalized parent-folder column or indexed SQL grouping. JTBD: When I open the sidebar for a large library, I want folders to appear quickly.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] list_folders uses SQL grouping / indexed derived folder metadata, not scan-all-in-Rust
- [ ] Perf test with 100k+ image_files completes folder listing under a defined budget
- [ ] Index coverage asserted for folder/path count queries
close_reason: Implemented, reviewed, merged to main (bd state reconciled 2026-06-03).

title: Bound file import/hashing/decode/embedding memory
description: Category: Scalability. Evidence: db_core/import.rs::sync_file reads full file into memory before hashing then decodes; db_core/gemini.rs reads full image + base64 for embeddings. Problem: large/malicious TIFF/PSD/RAW/GIF cause memory spikes/crashes. Recommendation: stream SHA-256, reject/downsample above byte/dimension limits before decode, apply provider size limits before cloud upload. JTBD: When I import huge or malformed images, I want Cull to fail safely, so I don't lose the session to a memory cliff.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Hashing uses streaming IO, never full-file Vec<u8>
- [ ] Import tests cover oversized/malformed/decompression-bomb fixtures
- [ ] Cloud embedding rejects oversize images with a user-visible error before full read/base64
close_reason: Implemented, reviewed, merged to main (bd state reconciled 2026-06-03).

title: Verify migration state with schema invariants
description: Category: Logic. Evidence: db_core/db.rs uses PRAGMA user_version as applied-check; run_migration_step records success when migration_already_applied(v) true, which only checks user_version>=v. Problem: high user_version with missing tables/columns/indexes treated as healthy. Recommendation: treat schema_migrations + schema invariant checks as authoritative; validate required tables/columns/indexes before normal open. JTBD: When Cull opens an old/partly-migrated DB, I want real schema verified, so I avoid silent corruption.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Fixture DB user_version=20 but missing image_tags fails preflight or is explicitly repaired
- [ ] Migration success rows not written merely because user_version is high
- [ ] Post-migration tests assert required tables/columns/indexes/FKs exist
close_reason: verify_schema_invariants() after run_migrations detects partial-migration corruption (high user_version + missing required table). Tests: 2 + 38 db regression. Reviewed: APPROVE.

title: Move blocking import/decode/DB work off async command paths
description: Category: Scalability. Evidence: single Arc<Mutex<Connection>>; import does file read/decode/thumbnail/source/sidecar/phash/color synchronously; async Tauri commands wrap blocking work. Problem: large imports block runtime, hold DB mutex, freeze UI/MCP. Recommendation: spawn_blocking/worker pool for bounded cancellable jobs; separate read/write SQLite connections with WAL+busy_timeout. JTBD: When I import a large folder, I want the app responsive, so I can keep browsing/cancel/monitor.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Importing a synthetic 10k-image folder does not block get_library_stats/list_jobs
- [ ] Thumbnail/phash/color/source run in cancellable background jobs
- [ ] SQLite uses WAL + busy_timeout; tests cover concurrent reads during import

title: Push MCP scope filtering and pagination into SQL
description: Category: Scalability. Evidence: mcp/tools.rs::list_images fetches limit*3 then filters in memory; scoped_images and rescan_sources hard-cap at list_images(100000,0). Problem: sparse scopes return short pages; >100k libraries silently truncated. Recommendation: SQL-level scope joins for folder/collection/tag, stable ordering, keyset/offset pagination, no global caps. JTBD: When I query a large library through MCP, I want correct paginated scoped results, so automation does not miss data.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Test DB >150k images returns scoped images beyond offset 100k
- [ ] Sparse folder/collection scope returns full pages without limit*3
- [ ] EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN / perf test confirms indexed scoped lookups
close_reason: SQL-level scope filtering+pagination via list_images_in_scope (folder/collection/tag union); removed limit*3 + 100k cap; scoped_images now tag-aware. Tests: db 33, mcp 109. Reviewed: APPROVE WITH NITS (applied).

title: Implement MCP tag scopes or reject them
description: Category: Security. Evidence: docs/mcp-remote-access.md advertises tags scopes; TokenScope.tags exists; services/tokens.rs::image_in_scope ignores tags entirely (returns false). Problem: tag-scoped tokens are a documented/typed contract the engine ignores. Recommendation: implement tag membership in shared helper OR reject tag scopes at creation. JTBD: When I restrict a remote token to a public tag, I want Cull to enforce that tag, so I expose only deliberately shared images.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Creating a token with tags either enforces filtering or fails with explicit 'tag scopes unsupported'
- [ ] If implemented, tag-scoped tokens reach tagged images and not untagged
- [ ] Unit tests cover folder-only, collection-only, tag-only, and mixed union scopes
close_reason: Implemented, reviewed, merged to main (bd state reconciled 2026-06-03).

title: Enforce MCP collection scopes consistently
description: Category: Security. Evidence: mcp/tools.rs::check_image_id_scope calls tokens::image_in_scope(scope,&img.path,&[]) passing no collection membership; services/tokens.rs::image_in_scope only allows collection access when image_collections contains the scoped id. Problem: collection-scoped tokens get inconsistent per-image authorization. Recommendation: one DB-backed helper loading path+collections+tags before authorizing any MCP tool. JTBD: When I issue a collection-scoped MCP token, I want all allowed tools to agree on scope, so I can delegate curation safely.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Collection-scoped token can list_collections/list_collection_images/get_image/set_rating for an in-scope image
- [ ] Same token denied for an out-of-scope image
- [ ] All MCP image-id tools call one shared scope-checker incl. collection membership
close_reason: Implemented, reviewed, merged to main (bd state reconciled 2026-06-03).

title: Audit P1 — High-priority hardening
description: ChatGPT 5.5 Pro repo audit (2026-06-03). High-priority recommendations. See docs/cull-audit-2026-06-03.md.

title: Replace generic bd preflight with Cull-specific preflight tiers
description:
## Why
`bd preflight --check` currently runs Go/Nix checks (`go test`, `golangci-lint`, `gofmt`, `go.sum`, `default.nix`) that do not apply to Cull. This creates false failures and hides the real Tauri/Svelte/Rust readiness checks.

## Acceptance Criteria
- There is a Cull-specific preflight entry point with quick, full, and release tiers.
- The selected command runs `npm run check`, `npm test`, Rust fmt/clippy/tests, and release-only checks at the right tier.
- Documentation tells agents whether to use `bd preflight`, `npm run preflight`, or another wrapper.
- Running the recommended preflight no longer emits Go/Nix checks for this repo.
acceptance_criteria: Cull-specific preflight tiers exist; docs point to the right command; generic Go/Nix checks are no longer the recommended readiness path.

title: Install and document bd/git hooks for Cull workflow
description:
## Why
`bd hooks list` reports every hook as not installed, and `.git/hooks` only contains sample hooks. The current workflow relies on agents remembering the landing-plane checklist instead of enforcing it locally.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `bd hooks list` shows the expected hooks installed locally, or a tracked setup script installs them safely.
- Hook behavior is documented for new agents and human contributors.
- Existing user hooks are preserved through chaining rather than overwritten.
- The hook plan separates quick commit checks from heavier pre-push checks.
acceptance_criteria: bd hooks are installed or safely installable; docs explain hook behavior; existing hooks are preserved; quick vs heavy gates are separated.
close_reason: Installed bd hook shims with chaining, added tracked Cull hook installer/runner/preflight scripts, documented quick pre-commit and full pre-push tiers, and verified bd hooks list plus local hook sections.

title: Dev workflow hardening: hooks, preflight, bd hygiene, and agent runbook
description:
## Problem
Cull has solid project-specific instructions and quality scripts, but the local dev workflow depends too much on memory. Hooks are not installed, bd preflight is running unrelated Go/Nix checks, open backlog items lack acceptance criteria, and release/license safeguards are not wired into every relevant gate.

## Success Criteria
- Cull has a documented quick/pre-push/release workflow with commands that match the actual Tauri/Svelte/Rust stack.
- bd hooks or equivalent local hooks are installed/documented and do not overwrite existing hooks.
- bd preflight or the replacement command reports Cull-specific checks, not generic Go/Nix checks.
- New and existing workflow issues have acceptance criteria.
- The release path runs the license/model policy audit before publishing.

title: Preview Display: shared Rust presentation state and commands
description:
Create the shared PreviewState foundation for both the native Preview Display window and the web-streamed Preview Display URL. Claude architecture audit pushed back on keeping native and web preview as separate state systems: they must share one canonical presentation state to avoid divergent focus, overlays, privacy controls, and streaming behavior.

Recommended architecture: store PreviewState in Rust-managed AppState behind a mutex or equivalent. Main-window focus changes update this state through commands/events. Native preview pulls the state on mount and subscribes to state-change events. Web clients read the same state through an authenticated preview server endpoint/stream.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance criteria:
- Rust defines a serializable PreviewState with focused image id, presentation mode/status, overlay/config fields, and updated timestamp/version.
- AppState owns the canonical PreviewState.
- Commands exist to get and update preview state.
- Updating PreviewState emits a state-change event for native preview consumers.
- Native and web preview issues depend on this shared foundation instead of duplicating state.
- TDD coverage proves default state, update/readback, timestamp/version progression, and safe concurrent access.
close_reason: Implemented Preview Display foundation, native window lifecycle, focus sync, dedicated read-only display surface, freeze/blank/presets, monitor placement/fullscreen, focused verification coverage, and tokenized one-way web stream. Verified with npm test, npm run check, focused Rust tests, and local Tauri release build.

title: Preview Display: Web Stream
description:
Build the web-streamed Preview Display layer on top of the shared PreviewState foundation. The user requirement now explicitly includes opening/giving a unique live URL for the preview display; this is not merely distant future research.

This remains security-sensitive: do not relax localhost-only static publishing behavior. Implement a dedicated preview streaming server with explicit lifecycle, token auth, privacy indicators, and one-way follow-the-curator semantics before considering Liveblocks or other collaborative products.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance criteria:
- App can start a Preview Display web stream and provide a unique live URL.
- URL uses a per-session token that is invalidated when the stream stops.
- Web preview follows the same PreviewState as the native Preview Display.
- Server lifecycle is explicit: start, stop, port fallback, active-state indicator, and graceful shutdown.
- Initial web stream is one-way via JSON/SSE or equivalent; Liveblocks-style collaboration is documented as future expansion, not a bundled dependency.
close_reason: Implemented Preview Display foundation, native window lifecycle, focus sync, dedicated read-only display surface, freeze/blank/presets, monitor placement/fullscreen, focused verification coverage, and tokenized one-way web stream. Verified with npm test, npm run check, focused Rust tests, and local Tauri release build.

title: Preview Display Web Stream: server and token spike
description:
Research and define the implementation details for the Preview Display web stream server before production code. This is now part of the requested Preview Display goal, but still needs a security/lifecycle spike before implementation.

The server should serve a unique live preview URL backed by shared PreviewState, not duplicate state from the native preview window.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance criteria:
- Spike recommends JSON polling vs SSE vs WebSocket for one-way follow-the-curator preview.
- Spike defines token generation, token lifetime, token invalidation, CORS/origin policy, and noindex/CSP headers.
- Spike defines server lifecycle: start, stop, port fallback, viewer count if feasible, and shutdown.
- Spike defines image serving path: source vs resized display JPEG, cache policy, and privacy risk.
- Spike explicitly defers Liveblocks/multi-user interaction to a later collaboration issue unless required by implementation evidence.
close_reason: Implemented Preview Display foundation, native window lifecycle, focus sync, dedicated read-only display surface, freeze/blank/presets, monitor placement/fullscreen, focused verification coverage, and tokenized one-way web stream. Verified with npm test, npm run check, focused Rust tests, and local Tauri release build.

title: Preview Display: define native-window state contract
description:
Define the implementation contract before coding the Preview Display MVP. This should pin down the dedicated window label/title, whether the display is route-based or window-identity-based, the event names/payloads used to sync state, and how the display fetches image metadata without importing the browser-only mock layer.

Recommended direction: use a dedicated Preview Display renderer and typed payloads rather than cloning Loupe behavior wholesale. The payload should carry focused image identity and presentation state; the display can fetch full image records through existing real Tauri invoke APIs when necessary.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance criteria:
- Contract names the window label/title and route or mode used for the display surface.
- Contract defines sync payloads for focused image, freeze/blank state, overlay visibility, and display preset.
- Contract explicitly keeps remote browser/iPad LAN viewing out of MVP.
- Contract documents how stale/missing images are handled.
- Contract references existing files likely to change: src-tauri/src/commands/window.rs, src-tauri/src/menu.rs, src/lib/menu.ts, src/lib/stores.ts, and the new display component/module.
notes: Claude challenge: consider separate read-only capability scope for preview-display instead of broad window-* permissions if feasible. The contract should explicitly state which IPC commands the preview window can invoke and avoid destructive/file-changing commands.
close_reason: Implemented Preview Display foundation, native window lifecycle, focus sync, dedicated read-only display surface, freeze/blank/presets, monitor placement/fullscreen, focused verification coverage, and tokenized one-way web stream. Verified with npm test, npm run check, focused Rust tests, and local Tauri release build.

title: Preview Display: build dedicated image-only display surface
description:
Create a dedicated Svelte display surface for the Preview Display MVP. It should present the current focused image on a dark, distraction-free canvas with predictable fit behavior. This is not the full Loupe: no crop tools, no grid chrome, no sidebar, no command bar, and no persistent app controls visible during client viewing.

The display surface should start with image-only rendering plus minimal transient status for empty/loading/error states.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance criteria:
- Preview Display window renders the focused image image-only on a black/dark background.
- Empty, loading, missing-file, and load-error states are legible but visually quiet.
- Fit-to-window is the default.
- The component uses Cull CSS tokens and does not hardcode arbitrary colors outside the theme.
- It reuses existing image path/convertFileSrc patterns and RAW fallback behavior where appropriate.
notes: Claude challenge: Preview Display should be read-only. Do not wire file operations, delete/move, crop, accept/reject shortcuts, or other curation mutations into the display window. It is a display surface controlled by the main window.
close_reason: Implemented Preview Display foundation, native window lifecycle, focus sync, dedicated read-only display surface, freeze/blank/presets, monitor placement/fullscreen, focused verification coverage, and tokenized one-way web stream. Verified with npm test, npm run check, focused Rust tests, and local Tauri release build.

title: Preview Display: add freeze, blank, and overlay safety controls
description:
Add presentation-safety controls for client or shared-screen sessions. The user needs to navigate privately in the main app without every intermediate image flashing on the external display.

Freeze keeps the currently displayed image visible while the main app changes focus. Blank hides the image entirely. Overlay visibility controls whether status/info is shown on the display surface.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance criteria:
- User can freeze/unfreeze the Preview Display from app controls or menu/command surface.
- User can blank/unblank the Preview Display.
- Freeze and blank state survive focus changes while the preview window remains open.
- Main app indicates when the Preview Display is frozen or blanked.
- Controls avoid destructive behavior and do not mutate library data.
close_reason: Implemented Preview Display foundation, native window lifecycle, focus sync, dedicated read-only display surface, freeze/blank/presets, monitor placement/fullscreen, focused verification coverage, and tokenized one-way web stream. Verified with npm test, npm run check, focused Rust tests, and local Tauri release build.

title: Preview Display: add MVP verification coverage
description:
Add focused test coverage for the Preview Display MVP. The risk is not database behavior; it is menu wiring, single-window lifecycle, cross-window state sync, and presentation-state correctness.

Coverage should follow Cull conventions: Rust tests for command/menu payload behavior, frontend tests for state/event contract, and E2E/browser tests only through tauri-mock.ts directly where browser-only testing requires it.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance criteria:
- Tests cover View menu action routing for Preview Display.
- Tests cover single-window reuse/focus semantics or the pure command helper that enforces it.
- Tests cover focus sync, freeze, blank, and error-state behavior.
- E2E smoke coverage opens the display surface where feasible in the existing test setup.
- No test imports cause src/lib/api.ts or app components to use tauri-mock.ts in production paths.
notes:
Claude challenge: add rapid focus-change and rapid open/close tests. Preview should show the final focused image after a burst, not intermediate stale state, and repeated toggles should not leave zombie windows.
Claude architecture audit first TDD slice: write Rust PreviewState tests first: default empty state, update/readback, timestamp or version progression, and concurrent read/write access. Then write frontend preview route tests and server/SSE tests.
close_reason: Implemented Preview Display foundation, native window lifecycle, focus sync, dedicated read-only display surface, freeze/blank/presets, monitor placement/fullscreen, focused verification coverage, and tokenized one-way web stream. Verified with npm test, npm run check, focused Rust tests, and local Tauri release build.

title: Preview Display: add View/Window menu commands and window lifecycle
description:
Add product-facing controls for opening and managing the Preview Display. The feature must be reachable from the View menu using the chosen name, Preview Display. Because it creates a native window, Window-menu affordances should be considered for close/focus/move behavior where they fit macOS conventions.

Implementation should prefer a dedicated open_preview_display-style command or focused wrapper over reusing the generic create_window command blindly. Reopening should focus/reuse the existing Preview Display instead of spawning duplicates.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance criteria:
- View menu exposes Preview Display controls.
- Selecting the menu item opens or focuses a single Preview Display window titled Cull Preview Display.
- Window lifecycle is deterministic: no duplicate preview windows from repeated menu actions.
- Menu event handling is covered in Rust/frontend contract tests where existing menu tests apply.
- API remains real Tauri invoke-based; src/lib/api.ts must not import tauri-mock.ts.
notes:
Claude challenge: use a semantic window label such as preview-display rather than generic window-N. Repeated View menu actions should focus/reuse the window. Prefer destroy-on-close plus recreate over hidden zombie windows. Add position/size persistence; keep monitor/fullscreen targeting out of P1.
Claude architecture audit: assign Cmd+Shift+P for Preview Display unless conflict tests prove it is unavailable. Keep Cmd+P free for Print/macOS conventions.
close_reason: Implemented Preview Display foundation, native window lifecycle, focus sync, dedicated read-only display surface, freeze/blank/presets, monitor placement/fullscreen, focused verification coverage, and tokenized one-way web stream. Verified with npm test, npm run check, focused Rust tests, and local Tauri release build.

title: Preview Display: synchronize focused image and curation state
description:
Wire the main Cull window to act as the control surface for the Preview Display. Grid/loupe/compare navigation in the main window should update the display window. Rating, decision, selected status, and basic image identity should stay coherent when changed from the main app.

Avoid brittle cross-window assumptions: the display may open after images are already loaded, may miss an event, or may need to recover from a stale image ID.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance criteria:
- Opening Preview Display immediately shows the current focused image when one exists.
- Changing focusedIndex or focusedImage in the main window updates the display unless the display is frozen.
- Rating/decision/status updates in the main app are reflected in the display payload or fetched state.
- If the image is removed, missing, or cannot be fetched, the display shows a quiet error/empty state.
- State sync is tested with unit-level store/event tests where practical.
notes:
Claude challenge: events across Tauri windows are fire-and-forget. Implement sync as push plus recovery: Preview Display should pull current state on mount/reconnect through a get_preview_state-style command or equivalent, and the main window should flush current state after a preview-ready handshake.
Claude architecture audit: consume canonical Rust PreviewState instead of relying only on frontend stores. Main window focus changes should update PreviewState; native Preview Display pulls it on mount and subscribes to preview state-change events.
close_reason: Implemented Preview Display foundation, native window lifecycle, focus sync, dedicated read-only display surface, freeze/blank/presets, monitor placement/fullscreen, focused verification coverage, and tokenized one-way web stream. Verified with npm test, npm run check, focused Rust tests, and local Tauri release build.

title: Preview Display
description:
Build a Capture One-style Preview Display for Cull: a separate presentation/viewer surface controlled by the main app. The main Cull window remains the control surface for grid, filters, curation, and navigation; the Preview Display shows the focused image on another screen, commonly an iPad used as a Sidecar/AirPlay external display.

Core direction:
- Name the feature Preview Display.
- Expose it from the View menu, with Window-menu support for native window management where appropriate.
- Prefer a dedicated display renderer over cloning the full Loupe UI.
- MVP targets a second Tauri window on the same Mac display environment.
- Remote iPad/browser viewing is a separate security-sensitive follow-up, not part of the native-window MVP.

Non-goals for MVP:
- No full layout editor for arbitrary display widgets.
- No LAN/browser remote viewer.
- No claim of reference-grade color approval.
- No mock invoke fallback in the app API.
acceptance_criteria: Epic is complete when Cull can open a Preview Display from the app UI, keep it synchronized with the main window focus/curation state, support client-safe presentation controls, offer configurable display information, handle external monitor placement/fullscreen behavior, and has a separate researched path for secure remote iPad/browser viewing.
notes:
Claude architecture challenge incorporated: native Preview Display should use a semantic preview-display window label, read-only capabilities where possible, View menu checkmark/lifecycle controls, push + pull state sync because emit delivery can be missed, and a preview-ready handshake to avoid losing the first focus update. Remote iPad browser viewing was split into separate epic imageview-8ii because it requires HTTP/WebSocket/auth/network policy rather than Tauri window IPC.
Claude architecture audit 2026-06-01: because web streaming is now an explicit goal, native Preview Display and web-streamed Preview Display should share one Rust-side PreviewState. Do not build a native-only Svelte state path that must be rewritten later. Add a P1 foundation issue imageview-52j.11 for canonical state/commands/events; native window and web stream depend on it. Use one-way SSE/JSON streaming for MVP web URL; keep Liveblocks/collaboration as future expansion.
close_reason: Preview Display epic completed: native/window controls, shared state, monitor placement, freeze/blank, configurable rail, histogram integration, docs, tests, and tokenized web stream are implemented and verified.

title: Fix low-contrast fallback tokens in Lineage and legacy surfaces
description:
Audit severity: P1 Major.

Location: src/lib/components/LineageView.svelte:270 through src/lib/components/LineageView.svelte:456, with related fallback patterns in src/lib/components/Toast.svelte:61 and other legacy components.

Finding: LineageView uses undefined design tokens such as --text-primary, --bg-elevated, --accent-warm, --bg-hover, and --accent, so the fallback colors are active. Several fallbacks are below WCAG AA on the app background: #666666 on #08080c is 3.48:1, #555555 is 2.68:1, and #444444 is 2.05:1. The current token --text-secondary on the app background is 5.11:1, so the low contrast is avoidable.

Impact: metadata, hint text, arrows, and secondary controls in Lineage can become unreadable for low-vision users and create a visual-system fork from the Tokyo Night app tokens.

WCAG: 1.4.3 Contrast Minimum.

Recommendation: replace undefined tokens and raw fallbacks with the root app tokens in src/app.css, or intentionally add missing tokens with AA-checked values. Audit Toast, ImportBanner, and other legacy fallbacks at the same time so inactive fallback paths do not encode inaccessible colors.
acceptance_criteria: Lineage text and controls meet at least 4.5:1 contrast in normal states; no active undefined CSS token fallback remains in Lineage; legacy fallback colors in Toast and ImportBanner are either removed or AA-checked; add a small token/contrast regression test if feasible.
close_reason: Implemented audit UX/accessibility/copy fixes with failing-first contract coverage; npm run check, npm test, and npm run build pass.

title: Expose settings and explorer toggle state to assistive tech
description:
Audit severity: P1 Major.

Location: src/lib/components/McpSettings.svelte:353, src/lib/components/McpSettings.svelte:360, src/lib/components/McpSettings.svelte:366, src/lib/components/McpSettings.svelte:371, src/lib/components/McpSettings.svelte:393, src/lib/components/StaticPublishingSettings.svelte:250, and src/lib/components/EmbeddingExplorer.svelte:1853.

Finding: several toggle controls are plain buttons whose accessible name is only ON/OFF, INDEX/NOINDEX, Preview, Text, or Labels. Most do not expose aria-pressed or switch semantics and are not programmatically tied to the row label.

Impact: screen reader users cannot reliably tell which setting is being changed or whether a setting is currently enabled. This is especially risky for close-to-tray, trash confirmation, auto update, HTTP server, auto-purge, search-engine indexing, and explorer panel toggles.

WCAG: 1.3.1 Info and Relationships, 4.1.2 Name Role Value.

Recommendation: use role=switch with aria-checked or button aria-pressed consistently. Include the setting name in the accessible name, for example aria-label="Auto update on" or aria-labelledby tied to the row label. Keep visible ON/OFF text only as secondary copy if useful.
acceptance_criteria: All toggle-like controls in settings, static publishing, and embedding explorer expose label plus state; screen reader output is understandable without surrounding visual context; source tests cover representative toggles.
close_reason: Implemented audit UX/accessibility/copy fixes with failing-first contract coverage; npm run check, npm test, and npm run build pass.

title: Normalize modal semantics and focus behavior
description:
Audit severity: P1 Major.

Location: src/lib/components/TrashConfirmDialog.svelte:35, src/lib/components/TrashConfirmDialog.svelte:38, src/lib/components/TrashConfirmDialog.svelte:40, and src/lib/components/McpSettings.svelte:306.

Finding: older dialogs do not consistently follow the modal pattern already used by AboutDialog, TextInputDialog, and CollectionTargetDialog. TrashConfirmDialog lacks role=dialog, aria-modal, aria-labelledby, and an accessible close-label. McpSettings puts role=dialog on the overlay, omits aria-modal and aria-labelledby, and its close button is only the multiplication symbol. Focus trapping and initial focus behavior are not evident.

Impact: assistive technology can miss dialog boundaries and titles, users may tab behind modal content, and close controls may be announced unclearly. This affects destructive and privacy-sensitive workflows.

WCAG: 2.1.1 Keyboard, 2.4.3 Focus Order, 4.1.2 Name Role Value.

Recommendation: centralize the dialog shell or align these components with the stronger dialog pattern in AboutDialog/TextInputDialog: role=dialog on the panel, aria-modal=true, aria-labelledby, close aria-label, Escape handling, initial focus, focus return, and no background tab stop while open.
acceptance_criteria: Trash confirm and settings dialogs expose title, modal state, close label, initial focus, Escape close, and focus return; tab order stays inside the open dialog; add component-level tests or source contract tests for the dialog attributes.
close_reason: Implemented audit UX/accessibility/copy fixes with failing-first contract coverage; npm run check, npm test, and npm run build pass.

title: Compare view panels are focusable but keyboard-inert
description:
Audit severity: P1 Major.

Location: src/lib/components/Compare.svelte:56 and src/lib/components/Compare.svelte:96.

Finding: the left and right compare panels are exposed as role=button with tabindex=0, but their onkeydown handlers are empty. They also do not provide an accessible name or active-state announcement.

Impact: keyboard users can tab to these panels but cannot activate them with Enter or Space. Screen reader users get a generic button with no useful side, filename, or selected-state context. This is a false affordance in a core comparison workflow.

WCAG: 2.1.1 Keyboard, 4.1.2 Name Role Value.

Recommendation: either make the panels real buttons where practical, or keep the container pattern with explicit Enter and Space handling, aria-label per side and filename, and aria-pressed or aria-current for the active side. Preserve the visible focus indicator.
acceptance_criteria: Tabbing to each compare panel announces side, filename, and active state; Enter and Space switch the active side; a focused active panel has a visible focus state; add a focused unit or E2E test for the keyboard path.
close_reason: Implemented audit UX/accessibility/copy fixes with failing-first contract coverage; npm run check, npm test, and npm run build pass.

title: Static publishing fullscreen image viewer
description: Generated static publishing sites should open image cards in a viewport-filling viewer with mobile swipe navigation and keyboard support for arrows, h/l, j/k, Home/End, and Escape.
acceptance_criteria: Image cards open an in-page fullscreen viewer instead of navigating away; viewer uses the best available image asset; previous/next navigation works by buttons, arrow keys, h/l, j/k, Home/End, and mobile horizontal swipes; Escape/backdrop/close exits and restores focus; static page remains accessible and mobile-friendly.

Verified with focused contract, headless Chrome runtime check, npm run check, npm test, npm run build, and npm run ci:rust.

title: Recover static publish local server when preview port is occupied

acceptance_criteria: Given the requested static publishing preview port is already occupied, starting the local preview succeeds on another localhost port and returns that actual URL.

title: Fix failing Help menu contract gates

title: Static publishing: expose best image assets with resilient metadata
description: JTBD: A recipient reviewing a Cull static canvas needs to open the best available version of each image and scan useful metadata without seeing broken or undefined values. Live audit of http://127.0.0.1:8000/ showed cards were image-only, not links, and metadata rendering assumed dimensions/rating fields were always present.

title: Static publishing: make exported gallery adapt cleanly on small screens
description: JTBD: A recipient opening the static site on a phone or narrow browser needs the share header, QR code, canvas snapshot, and image grid to remain readable without horizontal crowding. Live audit of http://127.0.0.1:8000/ showed the header/share row and compact grid could squeeze long URLs and cards.

title: Browser smoke: fix navigation and NSFW toggle failures

title: Command palette and workflow launcher
description:
Build a command palette for Cull that unifies keyboard shortcuts, menu actions, navigation, collection workflows, and future saved automations. The palette should follow proven patterns from Obsidian, VS Code, Raycast, and GitHub while fitting Cull's keyboard-first image curation workflow.

Core direction: create a shared command registry first, then have the palette, global shortcuts, app menus, context menus, and later CLI/MCP surfaces consume the same command definitions. Avoid a standalone modal that duplicates behavior from keys.ts and menu.ts.

Research constraints:
- Default shortcut should not be Cmd+P only; Cmd+P is Print on macOS and Quick Open in VS Code. Prefer Cmd+K for universal palette, with Shift+Cmd+P as command-only mode and an optional Obsidian-style preset.
- Keep existing / and Cmd+F behavior for natural-language image search.
- Pinning should cover commands, searches, and workflows, and should not be confused with the existing pinned import target.
- User-definable automations should be saved sequences of known Cull commands before considering arbitrary scripts.
acceptance_criteria: Epic is complete when users can open a fast keyboard-first palette, execute context-aware Cull commands, see shortcuts, pin frequent items, use recent/frequent ranking, and run at least one saved workflow built from registered commands.

AI generation metadata parsing — extract prompt/seed/model from PNG/EXIF
description:
Parse AI generation parameters from PNG/EXIF on import. No ML needed.

## Schema: image_generation_metadata table
- Columns: image_id, source, parser, prompt, negative_prompt, seed, steps, sampler, model, cfg_scale, raw_json
- FTS5 virtual table for prompt full-text search
- ~1ms per image (metadata only, no pixel decode)

## Crates
- png — PNG text chunks/iTXt parsing (Stable Diffusion, ComfyUI, A1111)
- kamadak-exif — EXIF from JPEG/WebP (Midjourney, DALL-E)
- serde_json — ComfyUI workflow JSON parsing

## Supported Formats
- Stable Diffusion A1111: 'parameters' text chunk with 'Prompt: ... Steps: ... Seed: ...'
- ComfyUI: 'workflow' and 'prompt' JSON in PNG text chunks
- Midjourney: Description EXIF field
- DALL-E: XMP metadata

## UI
- Show prompt in Loupe info panel
- Search by prompt text (FTS5)
- Filter by model, sampler
- Group by seed (find all variations of same prompt)

title: Perceptual hashing (pHash) for near-duplicate detection
description:
Compute perceptual hash on import for near-duplicate detection.

## Schema: image_perceptual_hashes table  
- Columns: image_id, algorithm, hash_hi (i64), hash_lo (i64), band0-3 (i64 for LSH)
- Band indexes for candidate filtering, Hamming distance in Rust
- ~5ms per image after decode

## Crate
- img_hash — configurable pHash/dHash/aHash

## Near-Duplicate Detection
- On import: compute hash, check bands against existing hashes
- If Hamming distance < threshold → flag as near-duplicate
- Show duplicate groups in sidebar
- Option to auto-skip duplicates on import
close_reason: Implemented schema v18 image_perceptual_hashes, DCT pHash generation on import/backfill, band-indexed near-duplicate lookup, Tauri/API commands, mocks, and Rust tests.

title: Florence-2 integration — API (HuggingFace/Ollama) and local ONNX for zero-shot detection + captioning
Research: additional embedding/metadata sources for richer image understanding
description:
Rich metadata sources for image understanding. Research complete.

## Tier 1 — High impact, easy to add
- **Color analysis** (k-means palette, no ML) — instant, cluster by color
- **Perceptual hashing** (pHash/dHash) — near-duplicate detection
- **PNG/EXIF metadata parsing** — extract AI generation params (prompt, seed, model, steps)
- **Aesthetic scoring** (CLIP-based, HPSv2) — rate image quality 1-10

## Tier 2 — High impact, needs model download
- **DINOv2** (300MB ONNX) — better embeddings than CLIP for visual similarity
- **SigLIP 2** (ONNX) — multilingual image-text search, better zero-shot than CLIP
- **PaddleOCR** (50-200MB ONNX) — extract text from images
- **InsightFace** (50-500MB ONNX) — face detection + grouping by person

## Tier 3 — Powerful but heavier
- **Moondream 2** (1.5GB via Ollama) — auto-captioning
- **Florence-2** (0.7-1.5GB) — multi-task: caption + detect + OCR
- **Google Gemini Embedding 2** (API) — 3072-dim cross-modal

## Implementation Priority
1. Color palette extraction (no ML, instant)
2. AI metadata parsing (no ML, instant) 
3. pHash near-duplicate detection (no ML)
4. DINOv2 embeddings (ONNX, better clusters)
5. PaddleOCR (ONNX, text in images)
6. InsightFace (ONNX, face grouping)

title: Multi-model embedding support — pluggable providers including Google, Ollama, OpenAI, local ONNX
description:
Support multiple embedding models via a pluggable provider system.

## Architecture
EmbeddingProvider trait in Rust with implementations for each backend:

trait EmbeddingProvider {
    fn name(&self) -> &str;
    fn dimensions(&self) -> usize;
    fn generate(&self, image_path: &Path) -> Result<Vec<f32>>;
    fn is_available(&self) -> bool;
}

## Providers (priority order)
1. **CLIP ViT-B/32 (ONNX local)** — current implementation, 512-dim, works offline
2. **Google Multimodal Embedding** — via Vertex AI or Gemini API, 1408-dim, best quality
3. **Ollama** — local models like llava, bakllava, moondream via ollama API (http://localhost:11434)
4. **OpenAI CLIP** — via API, requires key
5. **Nomic Embed Vision** — via Ollama or API, 768-dim
6. **Custom ONNX** — user provides their own .onnx file

## DB Changes
- embeddings table already has model_name column — each provider stores under its name
- User can have embeddings from multiple models simultaneously
- Embedding Explorer lets you pick which model's embeddings to visualize

## UI
- Settings/preferences for embedding provider selection
- Sidebar in Embedding Explorer shows model dropdown
- 'Generate Embeddings' button uses the currently selected provider
- Show which images have/don't have embeddings for current model

## Ollama Integration
- Check if Ollama is running: GET http://localhost:11434/api/tags
- List available vision models
- Use /api/embeddings endpoint with base64 image
- Models: llava, bakllava, moondream, llava-llama3

## Google Multimodal
- Use Gemini API (gemini-2.0-flash or multimodalembedding@001)
- Requires API key (stored in app settings)
- Best quality embeddings but requires network
notes: Release triage 2026-05-22: not a v0.1 source-release blocker. Local CLIP and Gemini Embedding 2 already cover the current embedding workflows; keep this as the next provider-abstraction/settings follow-up.
close_reason: Completed pluggable embedding-provider support for current app scope: CLIP, DINOv2, Gemini, OpenAI text embeddings, and Ollama text embeddings are exposed through backend provider metadata, generation commands, settings, availability checks, audit logging, and Embedding Explorer UI. Remaining true vision/custom provider work moved to imageview-57y because OpenAI/Ollama embedding endpoints are text-based.

description:
Object detection for auto-tagging images on import.

## Model Stack
1. **Primary: YOLO26 nano ONNX** (~6.5MB, ~10ms/image)

   - Run via ort crate (same as CLIP)

2. **Secondary: Florence-2 via Ollama** (zero-shot)

- Same EmbeddingEngine pattern: download model, load via ort, run inference
- Detection results stored in existing detections table
- Tags auto-generated from top-confidence detections
- Clusters in Embedding Explorer named from most common tags

title: Zen mode (Shift+.) — hide all UI chrome, show only images
description:
Zen mode — hide ALL UI chrome except images.

## Trigger
- Shift+. (period) toggles zen mode on/off

## What Gets Hidden
- Tab bar
- Sidebar
- Status bar
- All borders and separators
- Window title bar area (traffic lights still accessible on hover)

## What Remains
- Images only, on pure black background
- Keyboard shortcuts still work (just no visible hints)
- Mouse hover near top edge briefly shows tab bar
- Mouse hover near bottom edge briefly shows status bar
- Escape exits zen mode

## Use Case
Distraction-free image review. Maximum screen real estate for the content.
close_reason: Closed

title: Collections system — custom ordered sets with export to folders/formats
description:
Collections system — custom ordered sets with export to folders/formats.

## Creation Methods (all from Grid view)
1. **C key** — create collection from currently selected images
2. **Shift+C** — create from inverse (everything NOT selected / not rejected)
3. **B key** — enter Collect mode, Space adds images one by one in order, B again to finish
4. **Drag** — drag selected images to sidebar COLLECTIONS area to create/add to collection
5. **Templates** — create collection from a filter preset (e.g. 'all ★4+', 'all accepted', 'all PNG > 1024px')

## Smart Scenarios
- Select 5 in Grid → C → name → instant collection of 5
- Reject 3 with X → Shift+C → collection of everything else
- Filter ★≥4 → Cmd+A → C → collection of all high-rated
- Press B → browse and Space to pick favorites → B to finish
- Drag selection to 'New Collection' drop zone in sidebar
- Apply template 'accepted PNG > 512px' → auto-generates collection

## UI
- Collections in sidebar under PROJECTS/COLLECTIONS
- Within collection: reorderable grid with index numbers
- Drag-and-drop + Ctrl+Up/Down for keyboard reorder
- Grid view stays read-only

## Export
- Presets: web-ready, print, thumbnail-set, custom
- Template naming: {collection}/{index}_{name}.{format}
- Format conversion: PNG/JPEG/WebP with quality control
- Optional resize on export
close_reason: Implemented: schema, backend, sidebar UI, keyboard shortcuts (C, Shift+C, B collect mode)

title: Loupe view: fullscreen toggle via double-click, Enter, shortcut, Esc to exit
description:
Loupe (single image viewer) needs fullscreen support:

- Double-click thumbnail in Grid → opens Loupe fullscreen
- Enter key on focused thumbnail → opens Loupe fullscreen  
- Keyboard shortcut (e.g. F or ⌘F) → toggles fullscreen from any view
- Escape → exits fullscreen / returns to Grid
- Full window size by default when entering Loupe
- True macOS fullscreen (green button) also supported

Loupe already specified in design doc (view mode #3) with: pixel-level zoom, metadata panel, region selection, detection overlays. This issue adds the fullscreen entry/exit interactions.
